title: Relationship between social media use and reward processing across adolescent development
notes: FLIP from EXCLUDE. ABCD Study data confirmed. Examines social media use and neural reward responsiveness via fMRI (MID task) across 3 timepoints using RI-CLPM. No published peer-reviewed version found in database. Preprint on SSRN by Tomova, Foldes, Orben, Przybylski.

title: Longitudinal Associations Between Screen Time Activities and Working Memory Neural Signatures in Adolescents: Sex Differences and Contextual Moderation
notes: FLIP from EXCLUDE. Title indicates ABCD Study (longitudinal screen time activities + working memory neural signatures in adolescents with sex differences). No abstract available but title alone meets inclusion criteria. No published version found in database.

title: Associations between screen time and internalizing disorder diagnoses among 9- to 10-year-olds.
notes: UNSURE resolved to INCLUDE. Confirmed via web search: ABCD data (n=11,780), uses Youth Screen Time Survey with modality-specific measures including social media, texting, video chatting, online videos. Examines screen time and internalizing disorder diagnoses (DSM-5 K-SADS).

title: Unpacking Associations between Mood Symptoms and Screen Time in Preadolescents: a Network Analysis
notes: UNSURE resolved to INCLUDE. Confirmed via web search: ABCD data (n=9986), uses network analysis to examine different types of screen time and mood symptoms. Age-inappropriate screen content (mature games, R-rated movies) was key predictor. Lin, Eaton, Schleider 2020.

title: 24-Hour Movement Behaviors and Internalizing and Externalizing Behaviors Among Youth.
journal: The Journal of adolescent health : official publication of the Society for Adolescent Medicine
notes: UNSURE resolved to INCLUDE. Confirmed via web search: ABCD data (n=11,875), examines 24h movement behaviors including screen time recommendations. Meeting screen time guideline was significantly associated with lower internalizing/externalizing behaviors.
